issue_title: local variable 'message' referenced before assignment
issue_body:
Traceback:

```
$ conda env create
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "E:\Miniconda\Scripts\conda-env-script.py", line 4, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "E:\Miniconda\lib\site-packages\conda_env\cli\main.py", line 70, in main
    return args_func(args, parser)
  File "E:\Miniconda\lib\site-packages\conda\cli\main.py", line 208, in args_func
    print_issue_message(e, use_json=use_json)
  File "E:\Miniconda\lib\site-packages\conda\cli\main.py", line 227, in print_issue_message
    print(message)
UnboundLocalError: local variable 'message' referenced before assignment
```

From the code, looks like maybe `message` should be set to `str(e)` when the exception is `not in ('ScannerError', 'ParserError')`:

```python
def print_issue_message(e, use_json=False):
    from conda.cli import common
    if e.__class__.__name__ not in ('ScannerError', 'ParserError'):
            message = """\
An unexpected error has occurred, please consider sending the
following traceback to the conda GitHub issue tracker at:

    https://github.com/conda/conda/issues

Include the output of the command 'conda info' in your report.

"""
    if use_json:
        import traceback
        common.error_and_exit(message + traceback.format_exc(),
                              error_type="UnexpectedError", json=True)
    print(message)
```

issue_title: "No such file or directory" during multiple parallel activations
issue_body:
Nearly identically to #2837, I am also working in a clustered environment where multiple parallel processes are activating the same env simultaneously and I am getting "No such file or directory" regularly.

In fact running the following script, in 3 different terminals at once, will reproduce the problem within a few seconds:
```
#!/bin/bash
# Look for race condition in activating an anaconda env
# Run this in multiple windows at once and wait for one of them to crash.

ret=0
while [ "$ret" == "0" ]; do
    echo -n .
    source activate myenv
    ret=$?
done
```
The immediate solution (as #2837 did) would appear to be to add another exception to the exception [in install.py in symlink_conda_hlp at ~line 616](https://github.com/kalefranz/conda/blob/8b5bc4ec76075f2896d5a53d1b5e41a0194a9c11/conda/install.py#L616).  This time adding errno.ENOENT to the list.

So many exceptions to exceptions raises the question in my mind if re-symlinking is the right thing to do during activation.   If the symlinks can't be entirely removed, could this be replaced with just a check if the symlinks are there and a warning if somehow they've gone missing?  Keeping it read-only will avoid these race conditions.  (In fact, the above suggestion probably doesn't remove all the possible race conditions here.)  An arg to activate to force re-symlinking could be added to override the default of not re-symlinking.  Just some thoughts...

CC'ing NERSC admins who have helped me debug this: @rcthomas

issue_title: question: is there a way to do in-place arithmetic operations?
issue_body:
At the moment, I don't see a great way to apply a transform to a ``carray`` besides re-allocating all of the data, like:
```
my_ctable['f0'] = my_ctable.eval('f0 + 1', cparams=my_ctable.cparams)  # if you don't pass cparams, eval will return a column with bcolz's default cparams; same goes for the other kwargs to ctable's init
```
for more complex transforms (like logarithms, transcendental fcns, etc) the difference is smaller but for smaller computation, the above is much slower than say ``my_numpy_ndarray += 1``.
Plus if I'm applying some kind of transform to every column, that's worst-case duplicating all the data, which is a lot (hopefully gc cleans up before too long)

Is there a better way?

If not, any chance of adding this feature? I can see this being difficult or even impossible since data is likely stored compressed, but maybe there's a way? Or only supporting it for compression=0 types (feels yucky).

issue_title: scan helper not thread safe?
issue_body:
I hit a SerializationError when using the same client in a pool of multiprocessing processes and running scans.

```
pool = multiprocessing.Pool(processes=8)
pool.map(my_function, args)

def my_function(arg):
    resp = helpers.scan(my_app.es_client, index=..., doc_type=..., ...)
```
If I create a client inside each process, things work as expected:
```
def my_function(arg):
    client = Elasticsearch(...)
    resp = helpers.scan(client, index=..., doc_type=..., ...)
```
Am I wrong to expect to be able to do multiple scans with the same client?

issue_title: [Help] Argument groups no longer display correctly
issue_body:
Likely a side effect of PR #832.

```
Command
    az storage blob delete: Marks the specified blob or snapshot for deletion.
        The blob is later deleted during garbage collection.

        Note that in order to delete a blob, you must delete all of its
        snapshots. You can delete both at the same time with the Delete
        Blob operation.

Arguments
    --container-name -c [Required]: The container name.
    --name -n           [Required]: The blob name.

Storage Account Arguments
    --account-key                 : Storage account key. Must be used in conjunction with storage
                                    account name. Environment variable: AZURE_STORAGE_KEY.
    --account-name                : Storage account name. Must be used in conjunction with either
                                    storage account key or a SAS token. Environment variable:
                                    AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT.
    --connection-string           : Storage account connection string. Environment variable:
                                    AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING.
    --delete-snapshots            : Required if the blob has associated snapshots.  Allowed values:
                                    include, only.
    --if-match                    : An ETag value, or the wildcard character (*). Specify this
                                    header to perform the operation only if the resource's ETag
                                    matches the value specified.
    --if-modified-since           : Alter only if modified since supplied UTC datetime
                                    (Y-m-d'T'H:M'Z').
    --if-none-match               : An ETag value, or the wildcard character (*). Specify this
                                    header to perform the operation only if the resource's ETag does
                                    not match the value specified. Specify the wildcard character
                                    (*) to perform the operation only if the resource does not
                                    exist, and fail the operation if it does exist.
    --if-unmodified-since         : Alter only if unmodified since supplied UTC datetime
                                    (Y-m-d'T'H:M'Z').
    --lease-id                    : Required if the blob has an active lease.

Storage Account Arguments
    --sas-token                   : A Shared Access Signature (SAS). Must be used in conjunction
                                    with storage account name. Environment variable:
                                    AZURE_STORAGE_SAS_TOKEN.
    --snapshot                    : The snapshot parameter is an opaque DateTime value that, when
                                    present, specifies the blob snapshot to delete.
    --timeout                     : Request timeout in seconds. Applies to each call to the service.
```

The arguments now appear in alphabetical order with potentially multiple group labels.

issue_title: Bad checking of the `--pattern` argument
issue_body:
First of all, the `--pattern` argument description is giving very little
information: "used for files globbing"??  I had to try it out to see
how it's used.

Second, the way that it's implemented is probably going to be surprising
for most people.  I'm guessing that almost everyone would expect a
pattern like `x*` to match all files that begin with `x`, but it looks
like the pattern is actually matched against the full path that is
copied, and therefore a pattern like `x*` will either copy a whole
toplevel directory that starts with `x` or nothing at all if there is
none.

Related to this, it should be clear exactly *what* gets matched.  In an
attempt to avoid bogus matches in a list that iterates over a list of
globs, I prepended `*/` to each one (yes, that was a mistake) which
resulted in skipping a toplevel `index.html` file, and this is
regardless of specifying a full path as my `--source` or `cd`ing into
the directory and using a `--source .`.  So I'm guessing that this
pattern is matched against the full path *without* the prefix `--source`
directory (which is the same as the full container + `/` + blob name
*without* the part that is specified in `--destination`).  Summarizing
this in a short CLI description is going to be difficult, but it is
*very* needed.

Yet another bit that must be documented is the fact that `*` matches
*any* character, *including* `/`.  It would have been *much* better to
have `*` not match `/`s, and instead add a `**` pattern for that.

Given that there are probably scripts that rely on this behavior of
`--pattern` (at least the script that I wrote does so), a way to resolve
this would be to add some new `--file-pattern` that gets matched only
against the basename of files, and not against any directories.  Maybe
also another `--path-pattern` flag with `*` not matching `/`s, and `**`
that does.

issue_title: [Test] Need mechanism to thoroughly test module imports
issue_body:
Recently we released a module whose `setup.py` file was missing some necessary entries in it's packages list, resulting in a non-functioning build. The CI did not catch this because it only ensures that the wheel file builds and that `az` runs. This approach is insufficient because it is not guaranteed to trigger the relevant imports.

When we run a module's tests, we run the `dev_setup.py` script which doesn't rely on the packages list. 

We need to ensure that our CI system will, as best as it can, detect packaging errors like this. Possible solutions:

- include additional metadata in `setup.py` that runs certain commands after wheel installation to ensure that no import errors are thrown. This should be fairly easy to implement but the downside is that not every command which could be chosen is guaranteed to trigger the imports.
- Run the tests using a wheel installation instead of a dev setup.  Since the tests aren't included in the packages, this could be tricky, but would be the most thorough option.

issue_title: Importing msrest.exceptions is expensive
issue_body:
Importing msrest pulls in quite a few modules (indirectly), including, but not limited to, requests, in its __init__.py. The azure-cli is working on startup performance, and are looking at delay loading modules as much as we can. 

Being able to at least use the exception classes from msrest without taking the module load hit would be a very welcome improvement.

issue_title: Argument for ignoring certain files with the detect-private-key hook
issue_body:
It would be great if the pre-commit hook for detecting private keys could be passed an argument for files to ignore, something like `--ignored-files`.  My use case is having private keys in my testing code, in particular, testing asymmetric jwt decryption.

I can skip individual rules on a per-commit basis (using `SKIP`), but parts of my build pipeline run all files through the pre-commit hooks to check for errors. As far as I know, there is no way in pre-commit to specify files that are globally ignored. 

It would be a bonus if the hook trimmed the whitespace of the args after splitting them, so they can be pretty printed in a yml file:
```
hooks:
-   id: detect-private-key
    args:
    - --ignored-files
    - __tests__/my-test.secret.key
    - __tests__/my-other-test.secret.key
```

issue_title: btHeightfieldTerrainShape letting thin boxes through
issue_body:
I'm experiencing a strange behavior with btHeightfieldTerrainShape. It appears that boxes are slipping through the terrain, but only if they are elongated enough, and only in places (or with specific velocities).
I've made a little video showing the effect here: https://youtu.be/8DUJ1aoMd50
Square boxes aren't affected, but the more elongated the box becomes the more it seems to be.
CCD makes no difference (except for not allowing small square boxes falling completely through). As can be seen in the movie, the boxes seems to actually first collide with the terrain, only to then "slip" through.
Adding a margin to the height field has some effect, but will not be a guarantee.
I've also added a PR with a demo (the one shown in the movie) of the effect at https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/pull/1021
I've yet to look closer at the actual collision detection code; is this a known issue perhaps?

issue_title: Interest in a dm_control like API for continuous control tasks?
issue_body:
Is there any interest in a dm_control like API for pybullet's robotic tasks?

DeepMind recently released their suite `dm_control` which they use for continuous control. It features
a more customizable set of API which might be more useful for researchers working on RL for continuous control.

https://github.com/deepmind/dm_control

This this would allow for much more flexibility in engineering for state observations, how tasks are specified. They distinguish between domain and tasks. For example, given the same pendulum dynamics, we may have different sets of tasks to solve (swing up or something else). 
Currently, the way OpenAI Gym handles these things is not so flexible. I will probably write something recently, I am posting this here just to see if anyone else is actually interesting in this.

issue_title: Dimension of means_weight in GaussianHMM for multi-variate Gaussian
issue_body:
Hi, I was trying to set the means_prior and means_weight parameters in the GaussianHMM and got operands not broadcasting errors. Looking into the code got me wondering if this is the right approach for multivariate Gaussian. 

The model has 3 hidden states and 4 observed variables. Thus my means_prior is an array of size (3, 4); and to my understanding, the precision matrix will be size (4,4) for each state, so an array of size (3,4,4).
But the operation to update mean is an element-wise product instead of dot product, and it leads to not broadcasting errors:
(I'm referring to Section 7 of this tutorial for multivariate Gaussian priors: https://www.cs.ubc.ca/~murphyk/Papers/bayesGauss.pdf)

```
    def _do_mstep(self, stats):
        super(GaussianHMM, self)._do_mstep(stats)

        means_prior = self.means_prior
        means_weight = self.means_weight

        denom = stats['post'][:, np.newaxis]
        if 'm' in self.params:
            self.means_ = ((means_weight * means_prior + stats['obs'])
                           / (means_weight + denom))
```

Could you please let me know if I'm not understanding the prior parameters right? Thanks!

issue_title: make_mocked_request's default app should be a MagicMock
issue_body:
## Long story short
App is, among other things, a container for objects with an application-wide use. The default right now is to populate the `app` in the `Request` returned by `make_mocked_request` with `unittest.mock.Mock`, which doesn't support indexing, thus crashing when values in the `app` are accessed, unless MagicMock (or a dictionary) is explicitly put as `app` in the the call to `make_mocked_request`. I think the default should be to create the app as a MagicMock.

I couldn't test if that would brake any tests, because running tox was just spewing missing .h file errors at me and wouldn't run anything.

## Expected behaviour

```python
>>> from aiohttp.test_utils import make_mocked_request
>>> r = make_mocked_request('GET', '/bla')
>>> r.app['some_value']
<MagicMock name='mock.__getitem__()' id='139715764977336'>
```

## Actual behaviour

```python
>>> from aiohttp.test_utils import make_mocked_request
>>> r = make_mocked_request('GET', '/bla')
>>> r.app['some_value']
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
TypeError: 'Mock' object does not support item assignment
'Mock' object does not support item assignment
```

## Your environment
Aiohttp server: 3.3.2
OS: Kubuntu 17.10 x64

issue_title: Add support for setting cookies for individual request
issue_body:
## Long story short

A `cookies` kwarg that can be set with individual request providing cookies just for a single time.

## Expected behaviour

Aiohttp provides an interface that mimics requests, but lacks support for sending a dict of cookies with a single request. It's be nice to have an optional keyword argument `cookies` on sending a request, providing a way to pass in a dict of cookies that are added on top of the cookies stored in the cookiejar.

issue_title: URL Resolver methods should not reference private properties of parameters passed in
issue_body:
in https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py there are a variety of places where `_method` is pulled from the request instead of `.method`.  This in particular is causing me an issue when I try to pass a `RawRequestMessage` to my custom resolver (resolving each request of a google batch API call) as it only has `method` and not `_method`

see for example: 
https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py#L510
https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py#L702
https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py#L762

this is both bad design (referencing private attribute) and unneeded from what I see, they should just use `request.method`.

issue_title: Unable to influence sound level
issue_body:
I just realized that - short of muting the audio - I am unable to influence the sound level within pocket sphinx. I wrapped the microphone in two big pool towels, arecord doesn't register any level anymore and even a recording to file is close to muted, still pocket sphinx is happily trying to read the few air pressure changes that may make it through the towels. agc is set to none and agcthresh is set to 0. 

I had to push a softvol plug into the microphone stream and to apply -50db to finally defeat sphinx. Now - that doesn't make a lot of sense as those severely throttled audio packets don't carry a lot of information anymore - even if sphinx would re-amplify them again. 

There *should* be a way to override the amplifying capacity of sphinx in order to be able to adjust to different environments (e.g. where there are speakers in the background that shouldn't influence the STT). I would have though that disabling agc would do just that. 

I am overlooking something?

Michaela

issue_title: Feature Request: Enhanced Markdown Support For Graphs
issue_body:
Dear Jupyter-Notebook-Team,

I just got to know [an awesome atom markdown extension](https://github.com/shd101wyy/markdown-preview-enhanced) which includes many useful graphing tools not available yet in jupyter-notebook markdown (at least to my knowledge):

[copied from <https://github.com/shd101wyy/markdown-preview-enhanced/blob/master/README.md>]
- [mermaid](https://github.com/knsv/mermaid) to render flowchart and sequence diagram.  
	- code block with `mermaid` (or `@mermaid`) notation will be rendered by [mermaid](https://github.com/knsv/mermaid)  
	- check [mermaid doc](http://knsv.github.io/mermaid/#flowcharts-basic-syntax) for more information about how to create flowchart and sequence diagram   
	- ![mermaid](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1908863/23383956/5c8cb37e-fd0e-11e6-8a22-f3946841bbbd.gif)
- [PlantUML](http://plantuml.com/) to create multiple kinds of graph. (**Java** is required)  
	- code block with `puml` or `plantuml` or (`@puml` or `@plantuml`) notation will be rendered by [PlantUML](http://plantuml.com/).  
- [Viz.js](https://github.com/mdaines/viz.js) to render [dot language](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DOT_(graph_description_language)) graph.  
	- code block with `viz` (or `@viz`) notation will be rendered by [Viz.js](https://github.com/mdaines/viz.js).
    - add `engine:[engine_name]` at the first line of code block to choose different render engine. For example `engine:dot`. Engine `circo`, `dot`, `neato`, `osage`, or `twopi` are supported. Default engine is `dot`.   
- [WaveDrom](http://wavedrom.com/) to create digital timing diagram.  
	- code block with `wavedrom` (or `@wavedrom`) notation will be rendered by [wavedrom](https://github.com/drom/wavedrom).

I adapted the order to match my personal prioritized wish list.

Questions:
- Any plans to include such features?
- Maybe it is possible to create some nbextensions dealing with these features separately? Hints / suggestions how to do this?

issue_title: ignorefiles directory skipping bug
issue_body:
If a user uploads a list of directories and skips any the next directory is also skipped.
The problem is the code that walks directories:
https://github.com/Duke-GCB/DukeDSClient/blob/9d5473baa5bbd17a5f7c89ce8934a0370eb4c5d7/ddsc/core/localstore.py#L126-L132

`os.walk` requires skipped directories to be removed from`child_dirs` to avoid iterating over them.
This removal needs to be done outside of the loop above. https://docs.python.org/2/library/os.html#os.walk

issue_title: Superseded by official PyQt5 wheels?
issue_body:
Hey! I think this project was very useful back when PyQt5 didn't have official Windows wheels (thank you!), but I wonder if it's still needed? I can see various issues with it:

- People report PyQt/Qt bugs here because it sounds like an official PyQt thing.
- People find it on PyPI instead of the official PyQt5 and install it, and then either get an ancient Qt 5.3, or run into problems because of it not working (in the `#pyqt` IRC channel, we spent around 5h with someone having a "The specified procedure could not be found." error when importing PyQt5.QtCore, and it was really hard to find out that they didn't in fact install the official PyQt5.

So I wonder if there's still a reason for this to exist I'm missing? If not, maybe it should at the very least be deleted from PyPI, as Qt 5.3 is insecure, buggy, and in general not very useful nowadays?

Also, in either case, I think it would make everyone's life easier if this was clearly marked as unofficial and pointing to the official way to install PyQt via pip too.

issue_title: MultipartReader Instance Method (ReadNextSectionAsync) throws IOException: Unexpected end of Stream, Error.
Bug in HttpWebRequest when call an endpoint that returns 302
issue_body:
# Issue Title

When you call an endpoint that returns 302 the redirection url will have backslashes instead of forward slashes in "localpath".

# General

For example when you call Azure API using Fiddler or any other web debugging tool: "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/xxx-xxx-xxx-xxxx/providers/Microsoft.Commerce/RateCard" with property parameters, token, etc...

It will return 302 and redirect to: https://ratecard.blob.core.windows.net/ratecards/xxxx-xxxx-xxxxx-xxxxx/MS-AZR-0017P/USD/en-US/False/2015-06-01-preview/yyyy-yyyyy-yyyyy-yyyyy.json

No problem whatsoever...

But when you make the request using .NET Core 1.1/2.0 HttpWebRequest... 
The redirection tries to call something like that:

https://ratecard.blob.core.windows.net/ratecards/xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxx%5CMS-AZR-0017P%5CUSD%5Cen-US%5CFalse/2015-06-01-preview/yyyy-yyyy-yyyy-yyyy-yyyy.json

With encoded backslashes instead of forward slashes....

issue_title: Bundle dependencies along with the software
issue_body:
I am new to letsencrypt but i was reading through the documentation and found that the wrapper script `letsencrypt-auto` is actually determining the OS distribution and installing dependencies based on that. I would recommend to use something like `Omnibus` to package dependencies (https://github.com/chef/omnibus) or `Hashdist` (https://github.com/hashdist/hashdist) along with letsencrypt. 

There are some disadvantages to packaging dependencies (because you need to keep track of updates to all bundled dependencies) but maybe worth to consider.

issue_title: max_attempts in poll_and_ri should be per-authorization
issue_body:
`poll_and_request_issuance([authz1, authz2], max_attempt=1)` is the simplest example, where the code fails, because `max_attempts` counts total number of attempts for all authorizations, instead of counting per authorization. With default limit of 10, this causes problems in practice - usually when people are using more than 10 domains.

Core is not affected, because it doesn't use this retry loop. https://github.com/kuba/simp_le, however, is (and any other client using our library).

issue_title: Wrong symlink creating in mac.sh bootstrapper
issue_body:
I've just started experimenting with letsencrypt. I am also using MacPorts. When trying to run `letsencrypt-auto` I got following import error `raise ImportError("Unable to import lib%s!" % args[0])`.

In file `letsencrypt-auto-source/pieces/bootstrappers/mac.sh` there is this code responsible for creating `libaugeas.dylib` symlink:

```
# Workaround for _dlopen not finding augeas on OS X
  if [ "$pkgman" = "port" ] && ! [ -e "/usr/local/lib/libaugeas.dylib" ] && [ -e "/opt/local/lib/libaugeas.dylib" ]; then
    echo "Applying augeas workaround"
    $SUDO ln -s /opt/local/lib/libaugeas.dylib /usr/local/lib
  fi
```

`ln` command is  wrong bause this creates bogus symlink `/usr/local/lib -> /opt/local/lib/libaugeas.dylib`
1) Script should make sure /usr/local/lib directory exists (if not create it)
2) $SUDO ln -s /opt/local/lib/libaugeas.dylib /usr/local/lib/ (last "/" matter since we want symlink inside lib directory)

Possible fix:

then
    echo "Applying augeas workaround"
    $SUDO ln -s /opt/local/lib/libaugeas.dylib /usr/local/lib
  fi
```

issue_title: seek - needs poll
issue_body:
Seek fails unless a poll is done first:

```
from confluent_kafka import Consumer, KafkaError, TopicPartition
c = Consumer({
    'bootstrap.servers': 'localhost',
    'group.id': 'bob-group',        
    'default.topic.config': {
        'auto.offset.reset': 'smallest',
    }
})

c.subscribe(['topic-test'])
tp = TopicPartition('topic-test', 0, 10)
#msg = c.poll() #shouldn't be necessary think fault in client
c.seek(tp)
```
Results in:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "kafka.confluent.consumer.py", line 29, in <module>
    c.seek(tp)
cimpl.KafkaException: KafkaError{code=_UNKNOWN_PARTITION,val=-190,str="Failed to seek to offset 10: Local: Unknown partition"}
```


By uncommenting the line "msg = c.poll()", the seek will succeed.



System Info:

Ubuntu 16.04

confluent-kafka-0.11.4
kafka_2.11-1.1.0.tgz
zookeeperd   3.4.10-3 (installed via apt)

issue_title: Memory leak in cfl_PyUnistr_AsUTF8
issue_body:
In `confluent_kafka.h` this function has the following definition: `#define cfl_PyUnistr_AsUTF8(X) PyBytes_AsString(PyUnicode_AsUTF8String(X))`

The problems is that `PyUnicode_AsUTF8String` returns a new reference which is never released via `Py_DECREF` later on. Which means the return value of `PyUnicode_AsUTF8String` leaks memory.

I know a proper fix is hard, because the `Py_DECREF` must be deferred to the point where the result of `PyUnicode_AsUTF8String` is not used any more (considering also error cases ..).

I did not have problems with this leak yet, because the places where it happens are not triggered often in my code. However as soon as this is called frequently, for example `TopicPartition_str0` with an `errstr` set, it will have an impact.

issue_title: Parsed time zone wrong when adjusted at runtime
issue_body:
Because `tz.tzlocal` works out its offset at import time, there are problems when the time zone is adjusted at runtime.

We are running a test case for a time parsing function based on `dateutil` that parametrizes the local time zone over several values, e.g. UTC, CET.

When running the UTC test, with the TZ environment variable set to UTC and `tzset()` called, a time string indicating UTC (actually 'Z') is parsed. In this instance, the returned object has tzinfo set to `tzlocal()` because the parsed timezone ('UTC') matches the local timezone.

However, this is only true for the runtime local timezone. It wasn't UTC at import time but CET, which results in the test failing.

Example:

```
$ cat /tmp/testcase.py 
#!/usr/bin/python3
from dateutil import parser
import os
from time import tzset
os.environ['TZ'] = 'UTC'
tzset()
dt = parser.parse('2015-08-24T10:41:00Z')
assert dt.timestamp() == 1440412860.0
$ python3 /tmp/testcase.py
$ TZ=CET python3 /tmp/testcase.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/testcase.py", line 8, in <module>
    assert dt.timestamp() == 1440412860.0
AssertionError
```

issue_title: Distract the vector constructor and Vector type in parser
issue_body:
Most of the TL definitions use the `Vector t` type, but some of them (only one at the moment), use a `vector` constructor.

__Example:__
```cpp
// vector consructor:
future_salts#ae500895 req_msg_id:long now:int salts:vector<future_salt> = FutureSalts;

// Vector type:
messageActionChatCreate#a6638b9a title:string users:Vector<int> = MessageAction;

Currently all vector arguments are being serialized as `Vector` (with uppercase V), so it is impossible to check the CRC32 of the string:

```php
hash('crc32b', 'future_salts req_msg_id:long now:int salts:Vector future_salt = FutureSalts')
=> "94e2d547"

hash('crc32b', 'future_salts req_msg_id:long now:int salts:vector future_salt = FutureSalts')

issue_title: proposal: changing fields.Field's `**metadata` to `metadata=None`
issue_body:
**This is backward incompatible change.**

## pros

typo check is working correctly.

Sometimes, people that unfamiliar with marshmallow, want to add validation with typo.

e.g.

```python
class S(Schema):
    # this is typo. (correct keyword is `validate`)
    v = fields.Integer(validator=validate.Range(max=10))
```

Current marshmallow's fields.Field implementation is such as like below.

```python
class Field(FieldABC):
    def __init__(self, default=missing_, attribute=None, load_from=None, dump_to=None,
                 error=None, validate=None, required=False, allow_none=None, load_only=False,
                 dump_only=False, missing=missing_, error_messages=None, **metadata):
        pass
```

So, above typo is also valid schema definition. adding Range validator as metadata, and validation on deserialization is working without error when passing invalid data.

Sometimes, sometimes, the code such like this is passed our code review, then we are disappointed ours poor heads(this is careless human error).

But, if, metadata is just keyword argument(`metadata=None`), this typo-ed code is raising TypeError.

issue_title: Set name of an attribute to a value that is not a valid Python string
issue_body:
Hi. I use marshmallow to serialize/deserialize JSON and need to set the name of an attribute to include characters that are not allowed in Python (typically "#"). Example:

~~~
{ "#foo": 123, "100bar": 234 }
~~~

While I can override the attribute of a field, I have not found any way to change the name of a field in the output. I've tried using setattr, but that did not work for some reason:

~~~
class X(ma.Schema):
    #foo = ma.fields.Integer()   <---- this does obviously not work
    ...

setattr(X, "#foo", ma.fields.Integer())  <----- not sure why this doesn't work?

issue_title: NDArray: Putting in single ndarray in SArray creates wrong type
issue_body:
If you create an `SArray` from a single numpy array, it unpacks the first dimension as it should, but each element becomes an `array.array`. Not sure if this is a bug or the expected behavior, but it does seem odd to me:

```python
>>> tc.SArray(np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]]))
dtype: array
Rows: 2
[array('d', [1.0, 2.0]), array('d', [3.0, 4.0])]
>>> tc.SArray(list(np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]])))
dtype: ndarray
Rows: 2
[array([1., 2.]), array([3., 4.])]
```
I have tested this on both Python 2.7 and 3.6 with the same behavior.

For repro on Python 3 (#385 needs to be merged).

issue_title: Add Local Observer with TindyDB and HashFS
issue_body:
First, thanks for the great work on this library. I've been looking for something like this to help out with my data analysis pipelines and workflows and can really see the potential here.  

The first feature I found myself wanting was a local way to store observer information without the need to setup and run a mongoDB instance. 

One option for how to implement this is to have all the meta data stored in a json file using [tinydb](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/tinydb) on top as a lightweight substitute for mongoDB, and then storing the source files, resources and artifacts in a hashed file system with [hashfs](https://github.com/dgilland/hashfs). The hash based storage will simplify checking file existence and avoid storing duplicates, as well as provide an easy way to manage paths of files.  

I made a prototype implementing this before I noticed that release 0.7 has a few new observers including a file system one. Would this TindyDB and HashFS implementation still be of interest? I'll put together a Pull Request if so.

issue_title: the meaning of unknown in an optimization problem, and intermediate (nonoptimal) solutions.
issue_body:
1) If I stop Z3 before termination and get
unknown
(objectives
 (  (66 1039))
)
(model 
...

does this mean that it found a feasible solution (i.e., satisfies all the hard constraints), but not the optimum ? it makes more sense to me that the status should be 'sat' rather than 'unknown'. Perhaps there should be a new status 'sat but known is optimal'. 

2) could be great to give it the option to print non-optimal solution during run-time. Having the ctrl^c option is not always good enough when it is a long run-time: you do not know whether the solution is good enough to stop the engine or whether it even found a first solution. So while pressing ctrl^c tells you this, it also stops the process. 
A nice interface to this is e.g., the one in cplex. There you can give it a parameter CPX_PARAM_INTSOLFILEPREFIX with the prefix of the solution files that it will emit during the run.

issue_title: Identifying solver timeout from Z3's C API
issue_body:
I just received this bug report from @SeanHeelan  for KLEE when using Z3

https://github.com/klee/klee/issues/550

and it looks like we're hitting a case where Z3 is not being consistent about the message it returns when a timeout occurs.

Currently KLEE handles this like so.

https://github.com/klee/klee/blob/f997712d5ccac6714e57d87626e37bb762491784/lib/Solver/Z3Builder.cpp#L42

This is kind of a hack because we just look for the string `canceled` that comes from `Z3_get_error_msg()` and assuming it means solver timeout.

The reporter of the above bug is seeing Z3 returning `(resource limits reached)` which KLEE is failing to handle. KLEE of course needs to be taught to handle this but looking at this raises the following questions.

* Why is Z3 not returning a consistent message regarding timeouts?
* Why is there no C API that can be used to make it easier to check for this?
* Looking at `src/smt/smt_context.cpp` I see code that does this

```c++
            if (resource_limits_exceeded() && !inconsistent()) {
                m_last_search_failure = RESOURCE_LIMIT;
                return l_undef;
            }

```

This code is overwriting `m_last_search_failure` which `resource_limits_exceeded()` sets. This looks like it might be a bug (although `resource_limits_exceeded()` sounds like something that shouldn't change state... even though it does).

issue_title: Environment Name Can't Have Dots
issue_body:
See: http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/000095399/basedefs/xbd_chap08.html

Specifically:
> Environment variable names used by the utilities in the Shell and Utilities volume of IEEE Std 1003.1-2001 consist solely of uppercase letters, digits, and the '_' (underscore)

This means all dotted import names (like those used in the tests) must be replaced with an underscore.

issue_body: From [today's meeting](https://github.com/nteract/meeting-minutes/blob/master/2017/02-13.md), we talked a little bit about our use of metadata and where we could take things. Historically I've been against us introducing new metadata for fear of collision or a lack of maintenance around migrations (changes to the format, upgrades). issue_title: Namespaced parameters syntax improvement
issue_body:
Passing objects representing dicts is painful in papermill right now. You have to pass each value individually and catch the corresponding names inside the notebook. But when you have a large group of parameters all together (in say a json object) it'd be very convenient to specify those associated values on the input side but not inside the notebook.

Specifically a syntax along the lines of `-p foo.bar.baz value` could be passed to the command line resulting in the parameters cell a dict of form `"foo" = {"bar": {"baz": "value"}}}`. For repeated paths the dict could be enriched such that a second param of the form `-p foo.bar.baz2 value2` would combine to form `foo = {"bar": {"baz": "value", "baz2": "value2"}}}`. These parameters wouldn't need to share prefix paths, so `-p foo.bar2 baz3` would augment the top `foo` dict instead of the nested `foo -> baz` dict.

This would enable passing dynamic or many valued parameters that are together associated as individual inputs which are human readable and getting a clean hierarchy of dicts on the output.

Given the merge behavior of each assignment it could also be used to merge with existing dict variables to provide `foo = {'default': 'values'}` beforehand and have augmentation via the command line pass-through.

issue_title: Wrong encoding for reading notebooks on Windows
issue_body:
Hi,

we are unable to execute notebooks on Windows platform because the notebooks cannot be read due to incorrect file encoding. Notebooks are plain json files and as such encoded in UTF-8. However, the default encoding (on windows) is set according to the locale (in our case CP1250). Notebooks are read via `LocalHandler` in `iorw.py`. The function `io.open` is used, but no encoding specified and thus (incorrectly) set to CP1250. Would it be possible to fix this? There is the unfortunate problem of different approach in Python 2.7 and Python 3+ to handle file encoding. Thanks.

issue_body:
If I generate a path_group, run it and try to get the result in the same statement, a reference gets lost along the way.
This is a very minor bug and might also lie somewhere in simuvex, although I didn't find how it could. Maybe someone has an idea.

My bug is as follows. Calling:
```Python
res = p.factory.path_group().explore(avoid=0x400732, find=0x400743, strong_reference=True).found[0].state.posix.dumps(0)

    397         :rtype:     str
    398         """
--> 399         return self.state.se.any_str(self.get_file(fd).all_bytes(), **kwargs)
    400
    401     def dump(self, fd, filename):

ReferenceError: weakly-referenced object no longer exists
```

The post-mortem `%debug` gives some more information:
```
ipdb> self

As a workaround, I can simpy keep a reference to the path_group and explore in a second step, i.e.

Keep up the good work.

issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Maybe I'm missing something here, in which case I apologize. But it seems to me I cannot simply

1. load one or several MSG channels,

**Describe the solution you'd like**
See above.

I don't know satpy well enough to comment on that.

**Additional python or other dependencies**
No.

**Describe any changes required to the build process**
I think not.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

issue_title: Brush/Pen transparency weirdness
issue_body:
For various reasons, I've been using a pretty hacky method for applying transparency to aggdraw-drawn textures to surfaces in the Python package I maintain. This consisted of slicing off the 'A' value of any RGBA colours, creating a Brush and/or Pen with full opacity, and then applying the transparency value manually to the 'A' value for each pixel in the image using numpy. Last week, however, I figured out a way to fix the underlying problem that necessitated this workaround and decided to remove it and start using the regular Brush/Pen opacity values. 

Much to my surprise, however, this produced dramatically different (and wrong) results. See the following two images:

![screen shot 2018-01-11 at 10 58 54 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18648066/34832089-444a007c-f6bf-11e7-8f3b-73e92fcd6ee5.png)

![screen shot 2018-01-11 at 11 04 32 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18648066/34832091-47137d88-f6bf-11e7-849f-42858c0b41f3.png)

In both images, the semi-transparent circle over the '6' is supposed to have a fill value of (192, 192, 192, 128). The first is done with the old numpy transparency hack, the second with an aggdraw Brush. As you can see, the aggdraw one is considerably darker than it should be. 

Curious, I tried playing around with the Pillow image context aggdraw is drawing to, and sure enough, the fill colour of the fully transparent background affects the resulting colour of the aggdraw-drawn circle. For example, when setting opacity with aggdraw, drawing on a background with (255, 255, 255, 0) fill results in an actual fill (printing out the the pixel values to terminal) of (223, 223, 223, 127), whereas drawing on a background of (0, 0, 0, 0) fill results in an actual fill of (95, 95, 95, 127). When using the Numpy hack, it results in a background of (192, 192, 192, 128) regardless of what colour the transparent background fill has.

I'm guessing what's happening here is that aggdraw is trying to make up the difference between the background colour and the Brush colour as if it were an RGB surface without alpha, resulting in double-opacity. Is this something that's a quick fix in aggdraw, or is it a problem with the underling AGG library itself? I'd try my hand at a fix, but I have a lot on my plate programming-wise right now and I'm not too experienced in C.

Thanks in advance!

issue_title: /buckets/a/collections/b?limit="5" is allowed
issue_body: While investigating how best to implement the Kinto side of push notifications, I settled on using ETag as the "current version" to include in push notifications. I was worried about this at first, but @leplatrem pointed out that we support `?since="quoted Etag"`. That's interesting, because typically `"123"` should be a string rather than a number (because we parse values with JSON). But it turns out that not only do we support `?since="123"` (which we probably should, because it matches ETags), but we also support `?limit="123"` (which we probably shouldn't, because there's no string format for limits).

issue_title: Persistent memory backend? Load/dump fixtures?
issue_body:
Our use case is the following: for `kinto-http.js` we want an integration test to reproduce the situation where the history plugin was enabled after the creation of the collection. Since we use the memory backend, there is no persistence of data after change of configuration.

We have several strategies there:
* Mock server responses — but then it's not an integration test anymore
* Use a persistent backend like Redis or PostgreSQL — but then every contributor to kinto-http will have to run this backend for running the tests
* Add a way to `load` and `dump` data at the storage level to maintain the incomplete history — ie. not like kinto-wizard
* Make the memory backend persistent — ie. write a file on every write operation and load it if present on startup

Thoughts?

issue_title: Add BoB extension (or new BoB Featurizer) based on original BoB paper
issue_body:
Current BoB lists number of bonds by fraction (+ approximations and other fancy stuff). 
In [original paper,](https://pubs.acs.org/doi/pdf/10.1021/acs.jpclett.5b00831) BoB is a vectorization of the Coloumb matrix, with each bag sorted by magnitude and 0-padded (see Figure 3). 

A featurizer like what is described in the paper would be a dataframe-friendly extension of CoulombMatrix, since end result would be a single vector for each structure. It would be fitted to a list of structures in a similar way to current BoB, so that a given list of structures gives a list of vectors of equal length.

class Foo(models.Model):

issue_title: Make a Relay ConnectionField optimized to work with Query
issue_body:
Graphene has the `graphene.relay.ConnectionField`, which slices a list/iterable to build the edges inside the Relay connection.
But it doesn't play well with DB queries. [It calls `len()`](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-relay-py/blob/master/graphql_relay/connection/arrayconnection.py#L13) with `Query` doesn't have.

graphene-sqlalchemy should have a implementation of the ConnectionField that is aware of the sqlalchemy's Query and use it to slice directly in the DB.

For now, I'm using this workaround:

```py
class RelayConnectionField(graphene.relay.ConnectionField):
    @classmethod
    def resolve_connection(cls, connection_type, args, resolved):
        if isinstance(resolved, Query):
            len_ = resolved.count()
            connection = connection_from_list_slice(
                resolved,
                args,
                connection_type=connection_type,
                edge_type=connection_type.Edge,
                pageinfo_type=PageInfo,
                slice_start=0,
                list_length=len_,
                list_slice_length=len_,
            )
            connection.iterable = resolved
            return connection
        else:
            return super().resolve_connection(connection_type, args, resolved)
```

issue_title: Source Resize
issue_body:
**Use Case**
I want to resize the image of a model-field upon upload/save.

**Issue**
No intuitive solution found using sorl-thumbnail.

**Current Workaround**
As a workaround, I use the `ThumbnailerImageField` from `easy_thumbnails`.
Why? => Because it has a parameter to set the resize options upon upload/save.

```
from easy_thumbnails.fields import ThumbnailerImageField

class Feature(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True)
    image = ThumbnailerImageField(upload_to='features', blank=True, resize_source=dict(size=(40, 40)))


"""
Every feature should be represented with an image.
However to save disk space.. images should be scaled down to a fixed size.. in this example max-    width: 40px max-height: 40px (keeping the ratio).
"""
```

**Similar Issues**
There is a similar request/question for sorl on stackoverflow:
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/9354242/how-to-resize-source-with-sorl-thumbnail
The suggested solution does however save two versions on disk.. the original size and the scaled down size.. which is exactly what I want to prevent.
I want to save disk space by scaling down the image when uploading/saving.
No need to keep 10MB+ images.. when a thumbnail is all that is ever needed.

**Wanted Solution**
I would love to see the sorl ImageField (`from sorl.thumbnail import ImageField`) have an optional parameter to set the size. E.g.:

```
from django.db import models
from sorl.thumbnail import ImageField

class Item(models.Model):
    image = ImageField(upload_to='whatever', resize_source=dict(size='100x100', crop='center'))
```

issue_title: Linux x86 builds - OS dependencies issues
issue_body:
Currently, we have a handful of packages which fail for x86 builds on Travis.  Errors look like this: 
```
In file included from /usr/include/curl/curl.h:35:0, from /home/conan/.conan/data/Azure-C-Shared-Utility/1.0.41/bincrafters/stable/build/e77dff23066e0a9b35ee98e3dea7ddca38d50003/azure-c-shared-utility-2017-08-11/adapters/httpapi_curl.c:14:

/usr/include/curl/curlrules.h:142:3: error: size of array '__curl_rule_01__' is negative
   __curl_rule_01__
   ^
CMakeFiles/aziotsharedutil_dll.dir/build.make:1046: recipe for target 'CMakeFiles/aziotsharedutil_dll.dir/adapters/httpapi_curl.c.o' failed
```

@uilianries identified that the problem exists when building for arch=x86 on an x64 machine (such as travis), when the build requires linking to existing OS packages on the build machine.  This is because all the OS packages installed on the VM are the x64 variant, which will cause errors when you try to link to them dynamically during x86 compilation. 

One workaround would be to uninstall any x64 packages and re-install the x86 variant before we try to build. This would be ugly. 

Alternatively, docker should make this relatively easy to fix, since Docker images are already used extensively in conan_package_tools.  We just need any x86 build variants to automatically run on x86 docker images with all x86 OS packages installed. 

At least, this is our current line of thinking.  Does this sound like a good approach?  If so, how hard would it be to create copies of lasotes existing docker images but starting from x86 images?

issue_title: [MAC] Modify the MAC to store two channels; radioChannel and networkChannel.
issue_body: Currently, the MAC layer stores a mChannel value that represents both the channel set by otLinkSetChannel() and the channel to which the radio is currently set. A Discovery scan for instance, will use MAC::SetChannel() to change the channel while performing a discovery scan. When the Discovery scan completes the Discovery logic restores the channel to its original value.  This behavior is less optimal as it requires any new feature to implement a similar restore policy.  Instead it is proposed that the MAC implement the restore operation internally.  So that the network Channel can be set using a particular API and the radio channel can be set using a different API.  When the other module is done modifying the radio channel it can tell the MAC to restore its radio channel to the network channel using a well-defined API for that purpose.

issue_title: Upgrade credentials to JWT-based credentials if appropriate
issue_body:
Some APIs can benefit from using JWT-based credentials over OAuth 2.0-based credentials. Those APIs should upgrade to `jwt.OnDemandCredentials` if they are provided an instance of `google.auth.credentials.Signing`.

So, for example:

```python
credentials, _ = google.auth.default()

if isinstance(credentials, google.auth.credentials.Signing):
    jwt_credentials = google.auth.jwt.OnDemandCredentials.from_signing_credentials(credentials)
```

issue_title: Simple way to map C++ exceptions to existing Python exceptions
issue_body: The [documentation](http://pybind11.readthedocs.io/en/master/advanced/exceptions.html#registering-custom-translators) shows a simple way to map C++ exceptions to _new_ Python exceptions, however it does not show similar solution to map C++ exceptions to _existing_ Python exceptions. This should be simpler since it is not necessary to create the Python exception, but as far as I can tell the only solution to do this is to write custom exception translator. I'm interested mainly in the standard Python exceptions `AssertionError` and `NotImplementedError`.

issue_title: [regression] calling function with object of derived class doesn't work
Problem with format_descriptor and integral types in VS2015
issue_body:
This is kind of weird but I think it looks to me like a bug in `format_descriptor` or somewhere related.

I'm doing this inside a `.def_buffer(... { ...`:

`std::cout << py::format_descriptor<float>::format() << std::endl;`

and when I call the appropriate function from python, it outputs "f", as expected, and "d" if I plug in `double` as T. However when I plug in any other type (integral types I guess), like `unsigned char` or `signed int`, it outputs a blank string.

I've found the lines

`PYBIND11_DECL_FMT(float, "f");`

for float, double and bool, so the function is not defined for other types, however, there is a function

`template <typename T> struct format_descriptor<T, typename std::enable_if<std::is_integral<T>::value>::type> {`

which I think should get activated if T is an integral type, however, it doesn't seem to.

After a while of pondering over this I thought it might have something to do with the compile flags of pybind11, since in a "hello-world" repro in a C++ app, it prints the correct values, for example `pybind11::format_descriptor<unsigned char>::format()` prints `B`. However I commented all flags I could find like `/LTCG` and so on in pybind11's `CMakeLists.txt`, but there was no change in behaviour.

Initially, what happened was that my code works correctly for float/double, but when I put in unsigned char's in my data type and `pybind11::format_descriptor<unsigned char>::format()` into the `buffer_info`, python would complain with a `RuntimeWarning: Item size computed from the PEP 3118 buffer format string does not match the actual item size.`. Then, I found out what I described above, that in fact the format string is empty when it should be "B".

Sorry in case this is something stupid that I overlooked, but I spend quite a lot of time trying to solve this.

issue_title: Meta: Should we use an automated formatter to enforce our coding style?
issue_body:
Having to check each pull request for proper flake8 compatibility and readability can be tedious.

Can we improve our automated test suite to simplify that process?

An option would be to use an automated code formatter for that purpose, e.g [black](https://pypi.org/project/black/) or [yapf](https://github.com/google/yapf).

I've tried to run ``black`` on the current codebase, details are on the [rbarrois/blackify](https://github.com/django-ldapdb/django-ldapdb/tree/rbarrois/blackify) branch.

What is your opinion on this?

issue_title: WatchQuery does not respect the `base` of an object
issue_body:
In methods related to the Query class the `base` of the Object being used is passed along:
* [`execute`](https://github.com/kelproject/pykube/blob/master/pykube/query.py#L102)
* [`get_by_name`](https://github.com/kelproject/pykube/blob/master/pykube/query.py#L64)

But that [does not happen in the WatchQuery class](https://github.com/kelproject/pykube/blob/master/pykube/query.py#L157). Can be solved quite easily by copy/pasting the code to the function as well. Or more generally with some refactoring and calculating those params in a function or method (or even adding a `base_params` property to the class and using it all around.

issue_title: Include www variant by default
issue_body:
When configuring for example.com, the client should by default attempt to authorize itself for both example.com and www.example.com, and issue a certificate containing both.

Similarly, when configuring for www.example.com, the client should also attempt to authorize both domains.

We should only attempt do this if a DNS lookup reveals that both hostnames resolve to the same address.

issue_title: add the word `instead` to renew error
issue_body:
I tried to renew a certificate:

```sh
# ~/certbot-auto -d server.example.com renew
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log
Currently, the renew verb is capable of either renewing all installed certificates that are due to be renewed or renewing a single certificate specified by its name. If you would like to renew specific certificates by their domains, use the certonly command. The renew verb may provide other options for selecting certificates to renew in the future.
```

I'm not sure why my original command was rejected, I only specified one domain.
So, I naively tried to fix my command:
```sh
# ~/certbot-auto certonly -d server.example.com renew
usage:
  certbot-auto [SUBCOMMAND] [options] [-d DOMAIN] [-d DOMAIN] ...

Certbot can obtain and install HTTPS/TLS/SSL certificates.  By default,
it will attempt to use a webserver both for obtaining and installing the


cert.


certbot: error: unrecognized arguments: certonly
```

Suggestions:
1. Accept the input I provided (my guess is that the order `-d foo renew` confused renew and had I used `renew -d foo` it would have been ok)
1. Change `the certonly command` to `the certonly command instead`. I'm not really sure why I think the word "instead" would have queued my brain to drop the word renew from the command, but I think it would have.

In fact abc.xyz.net (not real domain here) resolves just fine, both by DNS A record and locally since it's in the hosts file on the webserver. And it resolves to an IPv4 address. issue_title: Improve usability of --external_listen_address option
issue_body:
When running multiple copies of raiden on a machine that is behind a fw/router, and using independent ports for each running instance, each instance can be started with `--external_listen_address host:port`.

The problem with the current semantics is that no matter which combination of `host:port`is passed, the internal listen port will still use the default of `40001`.

e.g: If I start two instances with external listen addresses at `x.x.x.x:40001` and `x.x.x.x:40002` both will try to listen on port 40001 (which makes the one at port 40002 unreachable). A workaround is also passing the `--listen_address` option with `127.0.0.1:port`.

To improve usability I would suggest reusing the current `port` passed on `--external_listen_address` for the local socket.

issue_title: PushManager.subscribe() needs applicatonServerKey option.
issue_body:
Please check the following links:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-thomson-webpush-vapid-02#page-6
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/PushManager/subscribe

I believe  option is necessary to send encrypted messages.

The server key is currently used for WebPusher.encode()

[The comment](https://github.com/web-push-libs/pywebpush/blob/master/pywebpush/__init__.py#L126) says

```

The server key is an ephemeral ECDH key used only for this transaction

```

However, I believe this key is not only for this transaction but also for the subscribe method.

issue_title: Execution of identical tasks.
issue_body:
We have discussed this topic before but perhaps we need to revisit:

Scenario:

```
input: for_each={'i': range(100)}
task:
   simulate()
```

In this case all tasks will be identical so they will share the same ID and signature file and cause trouble during execution. This is a bug that we should fix so we should either

1. disallow the execution of multiple tasks with the same ID, or
2. silently ignore the task that share ID with an existing queued tasks.
3. assign an different ID for this particular scenario.

I tend to solution 3 because this is more or less what users expect.

issue_title: Ivvi st_dacs_zero too fast
issue_body:
@AdriaanRol 
### Steps to reproduce
1. use `ivvi.set_dacs_zero`
2.
3.

### Expected behaviour
All dacs should sweep to zero, no faster than is allowed by their parameter settings 
`dac_step` and `dac_delay`

### Actual behaviour
instead the function `_set_dac` is used which does not listen to `dac_step` and `dac_delay`

###Proposed Solution
change `set_dacs_zero` to (untested):
```python
for i in range(self._numdacs):
    self.parameters['dac{}'.format(i+1)].set(0)
```

### System
**operating system**
Windows 7

branch derived from
**qcodes branch**
master
**qcodes commit**
latest merged qcodes master commit:
c67ced65e36fa38b4369275924ffb5e2df505cdd

issue_title: Multiple deploy keys
issue_body:
We currently cannot support multiple deploy keys. You need this, for instance, if you want to push to gh-pages for drdoctr/doctr and also to master of drdoctr/drdoctr.github.io (which we do want to do, to test it). See https://github.com/drdoctr/doctr/pull/190.

You can easily have two different deploy private key files, but the problem is that we encrypt that with a secret encryption key, which is stored in DOCTR_DEPLOY_ENCRYPTION_KEY. That key is generated when doctr configure is run, and is encrypted with the Travis public key. 

So we either need to:

1. Allow multiple encryption keys (like rename the environment variable based on the repo it is encrypted for).
2. Allow doctr configure to create multiple deploy keys at once, each encrypted with the same encryption key.

issue_title: min_freq=0 bug
issue_body:
**Noticed:**
```
>>>some_field.build_vocab(some_dataset, min_freq=0)
>>>padding_idx = some_field.vocab.stoi['<pad'>]
>>>print(padding_idx, '<pad>')
12 <pad>
```

Looks like <pad> is not equal to 1 which is not okay.

Printed `stoi` and `itos` as well:
```
>>>print(some_field.vocab.stoi)
defaultdict(<function Vocab.__init__.<locals>.<lambda> at 0x103f4f0d0>, {'<pad>': 12, '1': 2, '2': 3, '9': 4, '0': 5, '5': 6, '4': 7, '6': 8, '8': 9, '3': 10, '7': 11, '<unk>': 13})
>>>print(some_field.vocab.itos)
['<unk>', '<pad>', '1', '2', '9', '0', '5', '4', '6', '8', '3', '7', '<pad>', '<unk>']
```

**Possible reason:**
Counter subtract does remove the specials but puts their count at 0.
`counter.subtract({tok: counter[tok] for tok in ['<unk>'] + specials})`

**Possible solution:**
Throw an error if `min_freq < 1`

issue_title: Dimension order of batches
issue_body: It seems the BucketIterator produces batches in WxN dim order rather than NxW dim order, where N is the batch size. Given that PyTorch tends to want data with the batch dimension first, wouldn't it be better to transpose the output? Is there an advantage to the current output? (I suppose it's nice for RNN inputs, but I'm still trying to figure out why PyTorch wants RNN inputs in that form).

issue_title: Ignore hidden files in datasets.ImageFolder?
issue_body: For example, OS X likes to create ``.DS_Store`` files and they confuse ``datasets.ImageFolder`` (more precisely, the ``find_classes`` function) - it thinks this is one of the classes. Maybe it makes sense to ignore hidden files when determining classes that are present in a folder, what do you think? This particular case can also be solved if only directories were considered, not sure if it's ok to just skip regular files.

issue_title: Insecure Login Blocked - facebook
issue_body:
Hi, 
I used social-app-django for about a year with no problems.  Few days ago login through facebook stopped working.  I though it was due to facebook starting to force https on everyone. So I did setup my website to work with https. The error changed from domain related error to: "Insecure Login Blocked: You can't get an access token or log in to this app from an insecure page. Try re-loading the page as https://"

I have:
* updated social-app-auth-django to latest version.
* set Valid OAuth Redirect URIs to https://<my_domain>/oauth/complete/facebook/
* tried all of these: 
SOCIAL_AUTH_FACEBOOK_REDIRECT_IS_HTTPS = True
REDIRECT_IS_HTTPS = True
SOCIAL_AUTH_REDIRECT_IS_HTTPS = True

When I see the error from facebook, the url contains "redirect_uri" which starts with http://<mydomain> instead of https. And I believe this is the problem. But the question is - is it bad config server side, social-app-django side or facebook side?

issue_title: Bitbucket backend has no refresh_token method
issue_body:
```
from django.contrib.auth.models import User

user = User.objects.all().first()
social = user.social_auth.get(provider='bitbucket-oauth2')
social.refresh_token()
```
^^ raises `NotImplementedError: Refresh tokens for Bitbucket have not been implemented`

Please provide some way to solve this problem, because bitbucket auth tokens expire really often, they have an expiry time of 1 hour. Manually refreshing the tokens is really a hard job.

issue_title: [rllib] Improve model config documentation
issue_body:
I've been trying to use the model config as much as possible to avoid writing custom models, but often the documentation is difficult to understand.

For example, to understand the `conv_filters` option, the [documentation](https://ray.readthedocs.io/en/latest/rllib-models.html) says that the model config is documented in the [model catalog](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/master/python/ray/rllib/models/catalog.py). In the source code, the model catalog documentation for `conv_filters` says `"conv_filters", # Filter configuration`. I had to grep the source code before finding out that `conv_filters` is a list of `[out_channels, kernel, stride]` from [visionnet.py](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/master/python/ray/rllib/models/visionnet.py).

issue_title: Following PEP8 style guide

You can use `flake8` to check your Python code for compliance with PEP8, and other code smells.

issue_title: Create minimal web UI for debugging and for understanding performance.
issue_body:
We had an earlier attempt at a web UI, but no one used it, so we removed it in #554. Some problems with the previous UI:

- Installing it was difficult because it depended on node/polymer/bower.
- It required Python 3 which was a source of headaches.
- We had limited javascript expertise, so development was slow.

However, having a good UI is extremely valuable (e.g., see the UI's for Spark, Dask, TensorFlow, rllab). For example, we should be able to use the UI to answer questions like the following.

- My application failed, what went wrong?
- My application is slow, how can I make it faster?

We should create a simple UI to get something up and running that can help answer those questions. Note that the short-term solution may be different from the long-term solution. For example, maybe the "right" way to do it in the long run is React + D3. But it may still make sense to build a simpler version (e.g., based on a Jupyter notebook or Bokeh) to help flesh out the design and understand the requirements even if we end up rewriting it later.

cc @ericl @yifanwu

issue_title: Issue template improvements
issue_body:
Ever since the issue template has been split into multiple topic-templates, it feels like even more *bad issues* or issues without any useful information at all have been submitted than before. I didn't count them, so I may be wrong here, because a couple of *bad issues* have been created by a few people who were spamming the tracker lately, but still, I hope everyone can agree that the quality of new issues is not what was expected from this change. What is the reason for this?

**Is the template list too confusing?**
I'm not sure, but there are still lots of issues which are using the wrong template. Maybe the order should be changed and the descriptions be improved. It could also be the fault of some popular mobile apps. FastHub(-Libre) for example doesn't support multiple issue templates.

**Are the individual templates too complicated and overwhelming?**
I think a couple of things need to be removed, so the overall template size can be reduced, which will make it easier to understand. Maybe it's the HTML comment tags which are confusing. The first comment block on each template is pretty big. This could make people think that the whole template is just a single large comment and can be ignored. People who don't know HTML comments/tags or Markdown could also be intimidated or confused by this.

It's not a dramatic issue, but since there are a couple of new threads each day, I think this should be addressed, so that the amount of time unnecessarily spent on the issue tracker here can be reduced.

The problem is if I have a model like this:

```python
class Thing(psqlextra.models.PostgresModel):

And I do this:

```python
Thing.objects.upsert(

issue_title: `filename` not read from `Content-Disposition` for attachments to backup `Content-Type`
issue_body:
We ended up with a weirdly named attachment for a header we received that looked like this:

```
------=ALIBOUNDARY_59734_51bef940_59ce21a3_3685c
Content-Type: application/octet-stream
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="=?UTF-8?B?SU1HXzA0NDAuSlBH?="
Content-Transfer-Encoding: base64
x-aliyun-imageheight: 0
x-aliyun-imagewidth: 0
x-aliyun-localid: 6593C5A3-552B-47E6-84F1-359329A404B8
x-aliyun-voiceduration: 0

/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAAAAAAAAD/4QCMRXhpZgAATU0AKgAAAAgABQESAAMAAAABAAEAAAEaAAUA```

Thunderbird decodes it correctly as: `IMG_0440.JPG` but in our helpdesk system it's come up as: `part-2.so` which is the result of:
```
            if not name:
                ext = mimetypes.guess_extension(part.get_content_type())
                name = "part-%i%s" % (counter, ext)
```

I have no idea why this person's email client (or https://mail.aliyun.com/) encoded it in utf8 unnecessarily and didn't include the correct mime-type or a `name` param but hey... this is e-mail right?

I'm not sure where this bug lies, is it in the python email module or the get_email module of django-helpdesk... do we need a `name = part.get_param("name") or part.get_param("filename")` or is it not that simple?

As an aside I'm very confused by the email tests. Should they be using a library to construct emails? I'd be more inclined to have a folder of plain text real-world emails which we can load to test with. Rather than embedding them in the test code? Otherwise these kinds of weirdnesses (which seem to be inherent with email) are harder to test.

issue_title: User Testing Revelation
issue_body:
##  Few of the main issues I found while doing user testing.
1. The Square brackets [ ] in the 'Untracked Section' should be replaced with a '+' symbol, just to make them compatible with other sections.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13174154/29954623-422dc2a4-8ea7-11e7-8669-9ec103daeff2.png)

2. Users had a hard time understanding that files are already in the staged area, for example in the image below, users couldn't really understand that, 'sample.txt' & 'git_mainmenu_command.ts' are already staged. Maybe we should border the entire staged area with green color to associate that.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13174154/29954655-8d827c22-8ea7-11e7-89ff-01252080f770.png)

3. Almost nobody clicked the 'add_all', 'track_all', 'unstage_all' buttons (the buttons in the section header). They are somewhat hidden from a normal user's vision. Needs a keen eye to see them for now, we must make them more prominent. 

4. After the user hit the commit button, they were confused as the files instantly disappeared from the Staged Section. Probably we should make some animation which shows file icon moving up from the staging into the circular past commit section.

5. Many users tried to drag and drop of files from one stage to the other, looking back I think that's a pretty obvious choice. Maybe we should add the drag & drop facility from section to section.

6. (less priority) If we are doing the animation, we should also add animation for files moving from section to section (up as well as down)

issue_title: [Streams] Twitch Community support
issue_body:
For whoever is up for the task, I've done some digging into Twitch Communities and see if we can support them in Streams. And that answer would be yes.

For this we require the Client ID and a new accept header to access the Twitch community API
``Accept: application/vnd.twitchtv.v5+json``
from there we can grab the Community ID required to resolve it into the streams API, we do that by calling the following URL and grabbing the ``_id``
``https://api.twitch.tv/kraken/communities?name=<CommunityName>``
After that we call the Streams API
``https://api.twitch.tv/kraken/streams?community_id=<CommunityID>``

This should return a list with active streams for the active communities.

issue_title: Zipkin exporter sets incorrect localEndpoint.ipv4 for spans

It should instead be using the hostname/ip of the local host. The [python jaeger client](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-python) uses a good strategy for determining the local ip/hostname in [utils.py](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-python/blob/master/jaeger_client/utils.py#L61)

Thrift encoding breaks in python3
issue_body:
In python3, spans are unable to be serialized by thrift due to this line: https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-python/blob/master/jaeger_client/thrift.py#L55

In python3, the `encode()` function returns a `bytes` literal, which then causes the following error in thrift, which is expecting a `str`:
```
...
  File "/Users/nziebart/.virtualenvs/myapp/lib/python3.6/site-packages/jaeger_client/thrift_gen/jaeger/ttypes.py", line 146, in write
    oprot.writeString(self.key)
  File "/Users/nziebart/.virtualenvs/myapp/lib/python3.6/site-packages/thrift/protocol/TProtocol.py", line 121, in writeString
    self.writeBinary(str_to_binary(str_val))
  File "/Users/nziebart/.virtualenvs/myapp/lib/python3.6/site-packages/thrift/compat.py", line 41, in str_to_binary
    return bytes(str_val, 'utf8')
TypeError: encoding without a string argument
```

It can be fixed by replacing the `_to_string` function linked above with:
```
        if six.PY2 and isinstance(s, unicode):
            return s.encode('utf8')
        else:
            return str(s)
```

I can open a PR for this tomorrow

issue_title: sr_post - error message after posting a message
issue_body:
sr_post is able to post a message but getting an error message at the end. It looks like it had problem to close the socket or it tried to close a socket that already been closed.
...
2017-01-26 18:32:06,692 [INFO] post_log notice=...
2017-01-26 18:32:06,693 [DEBUG] Published1: xs_...
..
2017-01-26 18:32:06,698 [DEBUG] Closed channel #2
Exception ignored in: <bound method="" SSLTransport.__del__="" of="" <amqplib.client_0_8.transport.SSLTransport="" object="" at="" 0x7fb782325a90="">>
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/amqplib/client_0_8/transport.py", line 97, in del
self.close()
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/amqplib/client_0_8/transport.py", line 139, in close
self.sock.shutdown(socket.SHUT_RDWR)
File "/usr/lib/python3.4/ssl.py", line 807, in shutdown
socket.shutdown(self, how)
OSError: [Errno 107] Transport endpoint is not connected

..
works fine in flow_test, not reproduced yet.
need to try it out on an SSL one?
..
yes it appears that happen only to amqps.
..
workaround implemented in repo.... just suppressing the harmless message for now.
leave it there when in debug mode for now, so we look at it later.
..
It's just a message, and it's been hidden.

 was:  https://sourceforge.net/p/metpx/bugs/74/

issue_title: jsyaml.addConstructor fails (at least in browserified versions)
issue_body:
I’m trying to add a constructor for custom tags using

```js
jsyaml.addConstructor(
  '!sometag',
  function(node) { return "test"} );
```

This fails with "(Loader || _loader.Loader).addConstructor is not a function". I checked _loader.Loader using Firebug, and it indeed does not have that function. If it’s not a bug, do you have any hints on what I am doing wrong?
